chunk:
October 3, 2025
PLEASE NOTE:  Information Contained in this Letter is Confidential
Semester:  202610
Student Name:  Aidan Chan
Student Number:  T00760512
COMP 2210 S01 SEM
Aidan is registered with Accessibility Services (AS) and is eligible for academic accommodations.  As per Board 
Policy 10-0, Academic Accommodation and Services for Students with Disabilities, recommendations regarding 
accommodations are based on professional medical documentation and the functional impact(s) of the student’

ation and the functional impact(s) of the student’s 
disability. The provision of accommodation shall not lower the academic standards of TRU, nor shall it remove 
the need for evaluation or the student’s responsibility to meet the essential learning requirements of their 
courses and programs.  
Aidan qualifies for the following accommodations: 
Exam Accommodations
• Stretch/rest breaks in testing space (clock will not stop)
• Extra time for all quizzes, tests and exams: 1.75 extra time
• Accom

quizzes, tests and exams: 1.75 extra time
• Accommodations Expiry Date: November 2, 2025
Classroom Accommodations
• Extensions on assignments - to be negotiated with instructor: The student may occasionally approach you to 
request an extension on assignments. The student will need to contact you before the assignment due date to 
discuss alternative arrangements. Extensions will be determined on a case-by-case basis and are negotiated 
between the instructor and student based on the fact that

the instructor and student based on the fact that the student is known to have a condition that 
periodically affects their pace of productivity. It is not meant to be a blanket extension on all work. If an 
extension is required, it should reflect the nature of the assignment and the time frame required to do the 
assignment.
Lab Exam Accommodations
If applicable, the student and lab instructor should arrange a suitable time to discuss appropriate lab exam 
accommodations. Students and lab inst

te lab exam 
accommodations. Students and lab instructors may consult Accessibility Services to ensure the accommodations 
align with the lab environment. 
Practicum Accommodations
If applicable, the student and practicum instructor must negotiate a mutually convenient date/time to discuss 
appropriate practicum accommodations. Students and instructors may wish to consult with Accessibility Services 
at the beginning of the course to ensure that the accommodations match the learning environment.

the accommodations match the learning environment.
FYI
NoneStudent Responsibilities:
Students are required to speak with their instructors about putting accommodations in place.
If a course has any secondary instructors and/or practicum coordinators who will also be providing 
accommodations, students are required to share a copy of their accommodation letter.
Students approved for accommodations must send out their accommodation letters every semester using 
the Student Services P ortal. 
I

semester using 
the Student Services P ortal. 
If students would like to make any changes to their accommodations or if their accommodations will 
expire soon, they must connect with their AS Advisor.
If Students add a course or have an instructor change, they must send their accommodation letter(s) 
again to any new instructor(s).
Students with exam accommodations who would like to write in the Accessibility Services Test Centre 
must book their exam(s) with our Student Services Portal befo

heir exam(s) with our Student Services Portal before the exam booking deadlines, as per our 
exam guidelines.
To inquire about your eligibility for funding (i.e., for grants, services, or equipment), please visit the 
StudentAid BC web site or the applicable website for your province/territory.
If you’ve been approved for CAPER (alternate format textbooks), please visit our CAPER webp age for next 
steps.
If you’ve been approved for speech-to-text exam accommodations, please make an appointme

text exam accommodations, please make an appointment with our 
Assistive Technology Specialist for training. 
For more information, please visit our FAQs for Students webpage.
Instructor Responsibilities:
Instructors are required to speak confidentially with students about putting their accommodations in 
place.
If instructors have any questions or concerns about accommodations impacting the demonstration of 
essential course requirements, they must discuss them with the student and/or contac

y must discuss them with the student and/or contact Accessibility 
Services as soon as possible.
If students have exam accommodations and choose to write in the Accessibility Services Test Centre, you 
will be informed by email and provided with details about how to provide the test/exam to our office.
For more information, please visit our FAQs for Faculty webpage.
Please feel free to contact Accessibility Services if you have any questions.
Sincerely,
Accessibility Services 
www.tru.ca/as
